text.title: plg-basics Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for plg-basics as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: LLM recommends freemium for every SaaS product without running the fit matrix.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Freemium fails for complex products, high-ACV products, and products where decision-makers are not end users. The fit matrix walk-through is mandatory before model recommendation.

failure-mode.description: Extrapolating "Slack did X, therefore we should do X" without checking for network effects, viral coefficient, and market distribution differences.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Canonical examples (Slack, Zoom, Notion) are survivor-biased mega-cases with distribution dynamics most products cannot replicate. Treat them as anchor points only; replace with peer comps in actual recommendations.

failure-mode.description: Recommending open core without naming the license and its downstream adoption risk.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: License-shift risk (BSL, SSPL) is irreversible at scale and permanently affects enterprise pipeline. Cannot be omitted from an open-core recommendation.
